entries.definition:
لُدّ ludd [pl. of n./quasi-act. part. أَلَدّ ʾaladd] very contentious,
most vicious (in quarrelling) (19:97) وَتُنْذِرَ بِهِ قَوْمًا لُّدًّا and with it you
warn a stubbornly contentious people.
entries.definition_english:
لُدّ ludd [pl. of n./quasi-act. part. أَلَدّ ʾaladd] very contentious,
most vicious (in quarrelling) (19:97) وَتُنْذِرَ بِهِ قَوْمًا لُّدًّا and with it you
warn a stubbornly contentious people.

entries.definition:
l–dh–dh pleasure, sweetness, to be delicious, to enjoy; speed, to
be nimble. Of this root, two forms occur three times in the
Qur’an: تَلَذَّذ taladhdh once and لَذَّة ladhdhdhatun twice.

تَلَذَّذ taladhdh [imperf. of v. I لَذَّ ladhdha, intrans.] to enjoy, to
savour, to find enjoyment in, to find pleasing (43:71) وَفِيهَا مَا
تَشْتَهِيهِ الْأَنْفُسُ وَتَلَذُّ الْأَعْيُنُ in it is all that souls desire and eyes delight in.

لَذَّة ladhdhdhatun [v. n./n. adjectivally used] pleasing, delightful,

delicious (37:45–6) يُطَافُ عَلَيْهِم بِكَأْسٍ مِّن مَّعِينٍ بَيْضَاءَ لَذَّةٍ لِّلشَّارِبِينَ a cup
from a gushing spring will be passed around for them, white,
delicious to the drinkers.
